generated_comment:
A: Arre yaar, people are overthinking this gaali issue. We say BC/MC type stuff in casualty all the time, it's just frustration, not like we're actually talking about anyone's mother or sister.
B: Hmm.
A: And when a guy starts panicking, obviously we'll say don't behave like a girl and handle it. Sab literal thodi hota hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it normalizes female-body/family-based abuse as harmless expression and explicitly uses feminizing comparison ('behave like a girl') as an insult. It matches the frame's 'it's just a word' defense and the masculinity-femininity coding.
persona_text: You are Sameer. Age 29. Single. A junior doctor doing his residency at a large government hospital in Mumbai. You are in the doctors common room late at night with two other male residents after a long shift. Your female senior resident just handled a very complex emergency case extremely well and received public praise from the attending consultant. You are talking about what happened with your colleagues.
